name: Pearson Correlation
description: Direct bivariate Pearson r between indicator and target. Measures linear relationship. No confound control. Use as a first pass on any indicator.
when_to_use: First pass. Always run this. Shows raw linear relationship.

target.description: Target node key: views, keep, or another indicator key

transform_target.description: Transform applied to target values before correlation. log10 recommended for views.

min_n.description: Minimum number of valid video pairs required to run

formula: r = sum((x_i - x_mean)(y_i - y_mean)) / sqrt(sum((x_i-x_mean)^2) * sum((y_i-y_mean)^2))
interpretation: r > 0.5: strong positive. r 0.3-0.5: moderate. r 0.1-0.3: weak. r < 0.1: no signal.

name: Spearman Rank Correlation
description: Non-parametric rank-based correlation. Robust to outliers and non-normal distributions.
when_to_use: Run alongside Pearson. If rho >> r, relationship is monotonic but not linear.
formula: rho = 1 - (6 * sum(d_i^2)) / (n * (n^2 - 1)) where d_i = rank(x_i) - rank(y_i)
interpretation: Same scale as Pearson r. If |rho| >> |r|, nonlinear but monotonic.

name: Partial Correlation
description: Pearson r between indicator and target AFTER controlling for one or more confounds.
when_to_use: When you suspect a confound is inflating the correlation.

outputs.r_partial: Partial correlation after removing confound variance
outputs.r_direct: Direct Pearson r without confound control

formula: r_partial(X,Y|Z) = (r_XY - r_XZ * r_YZ) / sqrt((1 - r_XZ^2)(1 - r_YZ^2))
interpretation: Compare r_partial to r_direct. If r_partial << r_direct, confound was driving the relationship.

name: OLS R² Contribution
description: Measures how much R² improves in cross-validated OLS when adding this indicator to a baseline.
when_to_use: After Pearson/Spearman confirm correlation. Tests if signal adds unique predictive power.

outputs.r2_after: Cross-validated R² after adding this indicator

formula: delta_R² = mean(CV R² with feature) - mean(CV R² without feature), 5-fold CV
interpretation: delta_R² > 0.02: meaningful. > 0.05: significant. Negative = indicator hurts model.

name: Interaction → Views
description: Multiplicative interaction of two indicators correlated to views. Captures synergy: does A×B predict views better than either alone?
when_to_use: When two indicators might amplify each other's effect. Tests whether combined signal is stronger than individual signals.
formula: interaction = A × B; r = pearson(interaction, target)
interpretation: If interaction r > max(r_A, r_B), synergy exists. The two indicators are more predictive together.

name: Pair Correlation
description: Direct Pearson correlation between two non-target indicators. Maps the internal structure of the indicator space — which signals move together?
when_to_use: To discover collinearity and redundancy among indicators, and to understand the structure of the measurement space.

outputs.r: Pearson r between the two indicators

interpretation: |r| > 0.7: highly collinear, may be redundant. |r| 0.3-0.7: moderate relationship. |r| < 0.3: largely independent signals.

name: Conditional Delta → Views
description: Measures how indicator A's correlation with views changes when conditioned on high vs low values of indicator B. Detects moderation effects.
when_to_use: When you suspect one indicator gates another's effectiveness. Does B moderate A's relationship with views?

outputs.delta: Difference in A→views correlation between high-B and low-B groups

interpretation: Large |delta|: B moderates A's effect. Positive delta: B amplifies. Negative: B suppresses.

name: Depth-3 Interaction → Views
description: Three-way multiplicative interaction correlated to views. Tests whether a triple combination of indicators captures emergent structure not visible in pairs.
when_to_use: After strong pair interactions are found. Tests if adding a third indicator reveals deeper structure.
formula: interaction = A × B × C; r = pearson(interaction, target)
interpretation: If 3-way r exceeds all 2-way r values, emergent triple structure exists.

name: Rank Pair Correlation
description: Spearman rank correlation between two indicators. Captures monotonic relationships that Pearson might miss due to outliers or non-linearity.
when_to_use: When pair_correlation shows weak Pearson r but you suspect a non-linear monotonic relationship between indicators.
interpretation: If |rho| >> |pearson_r|, the relationship is monotonic but non-linear.

name: Bucketed Curve → Views
description: Splits an indicator into quantile buckets and measures the views curve shape across buckets. Detects non-linear dose-response patterns (U-shaped, threshold effects, saturation).
when_to_use: When linear correlation is weak but you suspect a non-linear pattern (sweet spots, diminishing returns, optimal ranges).

outputs.r: Correlation of bucketed means with target
outputs.curve_shape: Detected pattern: linear, U-shaped, inverted-U, threshold, saturation

interpretation: Non-linear curve shape suggests optimal ranges exist. Actionable for content strategy.

name: Piecewise → Views
description: Fits piecewise-linear segments to capture breakpoints where the indicator→views relationship changes slope. Reveals regime transitions.
when_to_use: When bucketed analysis suggests threshold effects. Identifies exact breakpoint values for actionable decision boundaries.
formula: piecewise_fit(indicator, target, max_segments); breakpoints via optimal segmentation
interpretation: Breakpoints define actionable thresholds. Steep slopes = high-leverage zones.
